title: Authors and Citation
text: Kim Cressman. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cressman K (2026). mseptools: Functions Mississippi Sound Estuary Program. R package version 0.2.2, https://github.com/CMEP-MS/mseptools.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mseptools: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program},   author = {Kim Cressman},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.2},   url = {https://github.com/CMEP-MS/mseptools}, }

what: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: goal mseptools provide R functions helpful Mississippi Sound Estuary Program MSEP-area researchers.

title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: can install mseptools GitHub following code:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("cmep-ms/mseptools")

title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: basic example downloading week’s worth salinity data USGS stations Mississippi Sound:
code: library(mseptools) dat <- get_mssnd_data(startDate = "2025-01-01", endDate = "2025-01-08") head(dat$data) head(dat$daily) head(dat$siteInfo)

title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: graph uses plotly, makes graphics interactive. addition hovering parts graph see exact values, can click legend entry remove (re-show) station; double-click show station. slider bar can narrow date range.
code: plot_mssnd_salinity(dat)

title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: function uses leaflet show USGS monitoring stations map.
code: map_mssnd_usgs(dat$siteInfo)

title: Functions for the Mississippi Sound Estuary Program
text: plot_mssnd_salinity() takes named vector colors; leaflet . common palette can created used functions follows:
code: stns <- sort(dat$siteInfo$clean_nm)  # static color palette colors_static <- as.character(khroma::color("roma")(length(stns))) names(colors_static) <- stns  # color function color_function <- leaflet::colorFactor(palette = colors_static,                                        domain = names(colors_static))  # plot and map with these palettes plot_mssnd_salinity(dat, colors = colors_static) map_mssnd_usgs(dat$siteInfo, color_function = color_function)

what: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend
title: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend
text: Adds legend salinity values bottom right leaflet map.

title: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend
code: add_salinity_legend(   map,   palette = "YlGnBu",   domain = c(0, 40),   color_function = NULL,   position = "bottomright",   opacity = 0.8 )

title: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend
text: map leaflet map object palette Character string specifying color palette name (default: "YlGnBu") domain Numeric vector length 2 specifying domain range colors (default: c(0, 40)) color_function Optional pre-created color function (overrides palette domain provided) position Character string specifying position legend (default: "bottomright") opacity Numeric opacity legend (default: 0.8)

title: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend
text: leaflet map object added legend

title: Add a salinity legend to a leaflet map — add_salinity_legend

what: Add salinity data points to a leaflet map — add_salinity_points
title: Add salinity data points to a leaflet map — add_salinity_points
text: Adds circle markers leaflet map representing salinity measurements, popups showing salinity value site information.

title: Add salinity data points to a leaflet map — add_salinity_points
code: add_salinity_points(   map,   data,   palette = "YlGnBu",   domain = c(0, 40),   color_function = NULL )

title: Add salinity data points to a leaflet map — add_salinity_points
text: map leaflet map object data data frame containing salinity data single date, generally created joining $daily $siteInfo components output get_mssnd_data() function, filtered single date.   Must include following columns: dec_lon_va: Decimal longitude station dec_lat_va: Decimal latitude station sal_mean: Daily mean salinity parts per thousand (ppt) clean_nm: Station name site_no: USGS site number palette Character string specifying color palette name (default: "YlGnBu") domain Numeric vector length 2 specifying domain range colors (default: c(0, 40)) color_function Optional pre-created color function (overrides palette domain provided)

title: Add salinity data points to a leaflet map — add_salinity_points
text: leaflet map object added circle markers

what: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
title: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
text: function cleans standardizes station names performing several transformations:

title: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
code: clean_station_names(name)

title: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
text: name character vector station names cleaned.

title: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
text: character vector cleaned station names standardized formatting.

title: Clean and Standardize Station Names — clean_station_names
code: stations <- c("Mississippi Sound near Grand Pass", "Rigolets at Hwy 90 near Slidell, LA", "EAST PEARL RIVER AT CSX RAILROAD NR CLAIBORNE, MS", "MISSISSIPPI SOUND AT USGS MERRILL SHELL BANK LIGHT", "MISSISSIPPI SOUND AT USGS ROUND ISLAND LIGHT, MS", "BILOXI BAY AT POINT CADET HARBOR AT BILOXI, MS") clean_station_names(stations) #> [1] "Grand Pass"                                #> [2] "Rigolets, Hwy 90, Slidell"                 #> [3] "East Pearl River, Csx Railroad, Claiborne" #> [4] "Merrill Shell Bank Light"                  #> [5] "Round Island Light"                        #> [6] "Biloxi Bay, Point Cadet Harbor, Biloxi"

what: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
title: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
text: Creates leaflet base map CartoDB Positron Esri tiles centered specified location (defaults Mississippi Sound).

title: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
code: create_mssnd_basemap(lng = -89.15, lat = 30.25, zoom = 9)

title: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
text: lng Numeric longitude map center (default: -89.15) lat Numeric latitude map center (default: 30.25) zoom Integer zoom level (default: 9)

title: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
text: leaflet map object

title: Create a leaflet base map centered on Mississippi Sound — create_mssnd_basemap
code: # Create a base map centered on Mississippi Sound create_mssnd_basemap()  {"x":{"options":{"crs":{"crsClass":"L.CRS.EPSG3857","code":null,"proj4def":null,"projectedBounds":null,"options":{}}},"setView":[[30.25,-89.15000000000001],9,[]],"calls":[{"method":"addProviderTiles","args":["CartoDB.Positron",null,"Positron (CartoDB)",{"errorTileUrl":"","noWrap":false,"detectRetina":false}]},{"method":"addProviderTiles","args":["Esri",null,"Esri",{"errorTileUrl":"","noWrap":false,"detectRetina":false}]},{"method":"addLayersControl","args":[["Positron (CartoDB)","Esri"],[],{"collapsed":true,"autoZIndex":true,"position":"topright"}]}]},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # Create a base map centered on a different location create_mssnd_basemap(lng = -88.9, lat = 30.3, zoom = 10)  {"x":{"options":{"crs":{"crsClass":"L.CRS.EPSG3857","code":null,"proj4def":null,"projectedBounds":null,"options":{}}},"setView":[[30.3,-88.90000000000001],10,[]],"calls":[{"method":"addProviderTiles","args":["CartoDB.Positron",null,"Positron (CartoDB)",{"errorTileUrl":"","noWrap":false,"detectRetina":false}]},{"method":"addProviderTiles","args":["Esri",null,"Esri",{"errorTileUrl":"","noWrap":false,"detectRetina":false}]},{"method":"addLayersControl","args":[["Positron (CartoDB)","Esri"],[],{"collapsed":true,"autoZIndex":true,"position":"topright"}]}]},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]}

what: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
title: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
text: function largely wrapper function dataRetrieval::readNWISuv, focused salinity 8 USGS stations Mississippi portion Mississippi Sound.

title: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
text: stations Character vector USGS station IDs. Defaults 8 USGS stations located Mississippi portion Mississippi Sound. params Character vector USGS parameter codes. Defaults 00480, corresponds salinity. startDate Date object character string YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults one month ago based system date. endDate Date object character string YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults current system date. tz Character string indicating desired time zone. underlying USGS function retrieves data UTC, wrapper defaults America/Regina - local standard time (adjust daylight saving time).

title: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
text: named list 2 3 components: data data frame raw unit-value (instantaneous) data USGS. siteInfo Metadata stations, extracted attributes returned data frame. daily (Optional) data frame summarizing daily minimum, mean, maximum salinity values site date.     included salinity data (Sal) detected returned dataset.

title: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
text: function downloads high-frequency data via USGS NWIS portal. specific Mississippi Sound salinity data processing well. function downloads high-frequency water quality data (typically salinity) USGS National Water Information System selected monitoring stations Mississippi portion Mississippi Sound. function returns raw data, site metadata, (salinity data present) daily summaries minimum, mean, maximum salinity values. function relies several external packages: dataRetrieval retrieving renaming USGS data via readNWISuv() renameNWISColumns() stringr pattern matching replacement variable names via str_replace() str_detect() dplyr data manipulation, grouping, summarizing lubridate date arithmetic default behavior focuses salinity (parameter code 00480), available parameters can passed.

title: Get data from USGS stations — get_mssnd_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get salinity data for the past month at default stations result <- get_mssnd_data()  # Retrieve water temp data from two stations  # View daily summary if salinity was included head(result$daily) } # }

what: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
text: wrapper around dataRetrieval::readWQPdata() simplifies data retrieval commonly used Mississippi Sound Estuary Program watershed, basin, coastal-county groupings. function handles multi-county queries internally binds results single data frame.

title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
code: get_wqp_data(   area = c("watershedComplete", "3coastalCounties", "6coastalCounties",     "basinCoastalStreams"),   sampleMedia = "Water",   service = "Result",   dataProfile = "resultPhysChem",   ... )

title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
text: area Character string specifying spatial area query. Must one : "watershedComplete" complete Mississippi Sound Watershed, HUCs 0317 0318. May return data Alabama Louisiana well Mississippi. "3coastalCounties" Jackson, Harrison, Hancock counties (MS) "6coastalCounties" Jackson, Harrison, Hancock, George,     Stone, Pearl River counties (MS) "basinCoastalStreams" MDEQ's Coastal Streams Basin: HUC 03170009 sampleMedia Character string passed dataRetrieval::readWQPdata() specifying sample media (default "Water"). service Character string specifying WQP service query (default "Result"). dataProfile Character string specifying WQP data profile (default "resultPhysChem", format includes method detection limit information). ... Additional arguments passed directly dataRetrieval::readWQPdata(), startDateLo, startDateHi, characteristicName, characteristicType (anything put 'Characteristic Group' WQP Advanced Query page narrow data types - desirable ones "Physical", "Nutrients", "Biological, Algae, Phytoplankton", "Inorganic, Major, Metals", "Inorganic, Minor, Metals", siteType.  NOTE: characteristicType WORK complex areas like multiple HUCs counties, even WQP site . Inside function, characteristicType option works area = "basinCoastalStreams".

title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
text: data frame containing WQP results requested area. Empty columns removed.

title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
text: function preserves Water Quality Portal (WQP) metadata stored attributes returned data frame, even empty columns removed post-processing. result, output can passed directly dataRetrieval::create_WQP_bib() generate appropriate data citation WQP query.

title: Retrieve Water Quality Portal (WQP) data for predefined Mississippi areas — get_wqp_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve data for MDEQ's Coastal Streams Basin for 2020 dat1 <- get_wqp_data(   area = "basinCoastalStreams",   startDateLo = "2020-01-01",   startDateHi = "2020-12-31" )  # Retrieve DO only data for the three coastal counties in 2020 dat2 <- get_wqp_data(   area = "3coastalCounties",   characteristicName = "Dissolved oxygen (DO)",   startDateLo = "2020-01-01",   startDateHi = "2020-12-31" ) } # }

what: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity
title: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity
text: Creates interactive leaflet map displaying salinity data USGS stations Mississippi Sound area. convenience function combines create_mssnd_basemap, add_salinity_points, add_salinity_legend functions single call.

title: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity
code: map_mssnd_salinity(data, palette = "YlGnBu", domain = c(0, 40))

title: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity
text: data data frame containing salinity data single date, generally created joining $daily $siteInfo components output get_mssnd_data() function, filtered single date.   Must include following columns: dec_lon_va: Decimal longitude station dec_lat_va: Decimal latitude station sal_mean: Daily mean salinity parts per thousand (ppt) clean_nm: Station name site_no: USGS site number palette Character string specifying color palette name (default: "YlGnBu") domain Numeric vector length 2 specifying domain range (default: c(0, 40))

title: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity
text: leaflet map object centered Mississippi Sound area (approximately   30.25°N, 89.15°W) station markers colored salinity values.

title: Create interactive salinity map for Mississippi Sound data — map_mssnd_salinity

what: Map USGS Stations in the Mississippi Sound — map_mssnd_usgs
title: Map USGS Stations in the Mississippi Sound — map_mssnd_usgs

code: map_mssnd_usgs(stations, color_function = NULL)

title: Map USGS Stations in the Mississippi Sound — map_mssnd_usgs
text: stations data frame station metadata, typically `stnInfo` data frame list generated `get_mssnd_data()`. Must include columns `station_nm`, `site_no`, `dec_lat_va`, `dec_lon_va`. color_function Optional. function mapping station names colors, one created [leaflet::colorFactor()]. provided, default palette generated using khroma package "roma" palette.

text: `leaflet` map widget displaying station locations.

title: Map USGS Stations in the Mississippi Sound — map_mssnd_usgs
text: `stations` data frame include `clean_nm` column, one   generated using [clean_station_names()]. map includes selectable   tile layers (CartoDB Positron Esri) circle markers popups displaying   station names USGS site numbers.

title: Mississippi Sound Watershed Boundary — msep_boundary
text: Outline Mississippi Sound Watershed, combination HUC4s 0317 0318.

title: Mississippi Sound Watershed Boundary — msep_boundary
code: msep_boundary

title: Mississippi Sound Watershed Boundary — msep_boundary
text: ## msep_boundary sf object, using CRS NAD83 (EPSG:4269), single polygon representing MSEP outline.

title: Mississippi Sound Watershed Boundary — msep_boundary
text: INTENDED ANALYTICAL USE. boundary simplified reduce object size. accurate boundaries, possible boundary configurations (e.g. portion watershed inside state MS), see msepBoundaries package: https://github.com/CMEP-MS/msepBoundaries

what: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2010-2024 — mssnd_salinity
title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2010-2024 — mssnd_salinity
text: Salinity data 8 Mississippi Sound USGS stations. available readings downloaded USGS NWIS API using `get_mssnd_salinity()`, wrapper `dataRetrieval::readNWISuv`. Readings also processed daily min, mean, max. Station metadata provided `siteInfo`.

title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2010-2024 — mssnd_salinity
code: mssnd_salinity

title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2010-2024 — mssnd_salinity
text: ## `mssnd_salinity` list containing 3 data frames: data data frame salinity readings siteInfo data frame station metadata, including lat/long coordinates   cleaned names. Cleaned names factor, ordered west east. daily data frame min, max, mean salinity per day station.

what: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2019 — mssnd_salinity2019
title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2019 — mssnd_salinity2019
text: Salinity data 8 Mississippi Sound USGS stations, subsetted `mssnd_salinity` include year 2019. available readings downloaded USGS NWIS API using `get_mssnd_salinity()`, wrapper `dataRetrieval::readNWISuv`. Readings also processed daily min, mean, max. Station metadata provided `siteInfo`.

title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2019 — mssnd_salinity2019
code: mssnd_salinity2019

title: Mississippi Sound Salinity Data from 2019 — mssnd_salinity2019
text: ## `mssnd_salinity` list containing 3 data frames: data data frame salinity readings siteInfo data frame station metadata, including lat/long coordinates   cleaned names. Cleaned names factor, ordered west east. daily data frame min, max, mean salinity per day station.

what: Plot Mississippi Sound Salinity Data — plot_mssnd_salinity
title: Plot Mississippi Sound Salinity Data — plot_mssnd_salinity
text: Creates plotly visualization salinity data USGS monitoring stations Mississippi portion Mississippi Sound. plot stacked default, instantaneous values top graph daily means bottom. range slider bottom allow viewing smaller portion time.

title: Plot Mississippi Sound Salinity Data — plot_mssnd_salinity
code: plot_mssnd_salinity(data, nrow = 2, colors = NULL)

title: Plot Mississippi Sound Salinity Data — plot_mssnd_salinity
text: data list three required elements: data (instantaneous measurements), daily (daily averages), siteInfo (station metadata). typically output get_mssnd_data() function. nrow Integer. Number rows subplot layout. Default 2 (instantaneous data top, daily means bottom). Change 1 prefer graphs side--side. colors Character vector color codes use different stations. Can named vector. NULL (default), khroma package's "roma" scheme used.

title: Plot Mississippi Sound Salinity Data — plot_mssnd_salinity
text: plotly object interactive time series plots salinity data. top plot shows   instantaneous measurements, bottom plot shows daily means. plots share x y axes.   date range slider included easier navigation time series.

what: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
text: function restores attributes lost empty columns removed Water Quality Portal (WQP) data download. intended used applying janitor::remove_empty("cols") (similar) WQP data frame, drops important metadata stored attributes (e.g., site information, characteristic metadata, request URLs).

title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
code: retain_wqp_attributes(data_full, data_reduced)

title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
text: data_full data frame returned directly Water Quality Portal (WQP) download function dataRetrieval::readWQPdata(), containing full set WQP attributes. data_reduced data frame derived data_full empty columns removed (e.g., via janitor::remove_empty("cols")), lost original attributes.

title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
text: data frame columns data_reduced   attributes data_full restored missing. returned   object preserves WQP metadata site information, variable   descriptions, request details.

title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
text: function copies attributes original full data frame re-attaches attributes missing reduced data frame, returning data frame fewer columns original WQP metadata intact.

title: Restore WQP attributes after column removal — retain_wqp_attributes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ full <- get_wqp_data(...) reduced <- janitor::remove_empty(full, "cols") restored <- retain_wqp_attributes(full, reduced) } # }

title: mseptools 0.2.2
text: added tests plotting mapping functions

title: mseptools 0.2.1
text: modified multiple HUC selection inside get_wqp_data(). (learned bump versions use git tags.)

title: mseptools 0.2.0
text: added function get_wqp_data() pull data Water Quality Portal MSEP areas. wrapper function dataRetrieval::readWQPdata(). added function retain_wqp_attributes() keep attributes/metadata using functions strip away attributes (like janitor::remove_empty()). Mostly helper function get_wqp_data(), useful situations. added unit tests functions.

title: mseptools 0.1.0
text: created.
